text.title: AIOps Anomaly + Auto-remediation Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `AIOps Anomaly + Auto-remediation Playbook` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: An anomaly MUST be confirmed by ≥ 2 independent signals (metric + log OR metric + trace) before it is promoted to an incident.

rationale.source: Google SRE workbook + AIOps vendor case studies

rule.rationale: Single-signal anomalies are noisy; multi-signal convergence is the testable promotion floor.

rule.statement: Auto-remediation MUST be classified per action into one of: recommend-only, human-approved, autonomous (low-risk reversible only). Cross-tier moves require a named approver.
rule.rationale: Untiered auto-remediation has destroyed prod data; explicit trust tiers contain blast radius.

rule.statement: Anomaly-promotion precision (true incidents / promoted anomalies) MUST be > 60% before any tier promotes to autonomous.
rule.rationale: Below 60% precision the noise drowns the signal; autonomy on noisy signal causes outages.

rule.statement: Anomalies within 30 minutes of a deploy / config change MUST receive higher weight in incident promotion.
rule.rationale: Most outages cluster around recent change; weighting matches operator intuition.

rule.statement: Anomaly thresholds + remediation tier assignments MUST be reviewed quarterly with outcome metrics (MTTR delta, false-positive rate).
rule.rationale: Static AIOps configs rot; quarterly review keeps it aligned with current traffic shape.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
